data.context_remarks: The school eye health program in the intervention areas are implemented by the Ministries of Education and Health with support from Sightsavers. Sightsavers’ school health integrated program (SHIP) objective is to improve the health, and educational outcomes of school aged children in Liberia. Working in partnership with the Ministry of Health and Ministry of Education to trains schoolteachers to deliver eye vision screening and provide deworming tablets to school aged children in the intervention areas.

1.4 Key stakeholders and coordination:  Which ministry/ministries is/are 
responsible for school health and/or 
school eye health in the country?
 If multiple, is there a primary or 
lead Ministry?Ministries of Education and Health. The Ministry of Education is the primary lead.

Is there a designated unit or officer 
for school health and/or school eye 
health within the ministry structure?: The department of school health at both Ministries which is headed by a Director. At the Ministry of Education, this department is under the Student Personnel Services and at the Ministry of Health under Community Health.

1.4 Key stakeholders and coordination:  Is there any mechanism for  coordination of school health and/
 or school eye health between the 
ministries of health and education?: The collaboration of the Eye Health Technical Working Group, which involves the Ministries of Education and Health for integrated eye health services, especially the SHIP project concept, in their respective sectoral plans, ensuring that these plans are robustly monitored in order to reduce the impact of visual ailments for the Liberian population especially in rural communities.

The National Social Protection Policy and Strategy is a 5-year national wide document developed by the Government of Liberia aimed at building a social protection system that tackles extreme poverty, vulnerability and inequality in Liberia whilst contributing to economic growth and peace and security. The Social Protection policy provides a unique opportunity to make a difference in the lives of the extremely poor and most vulnerable segments of the Liberian population, by integrating them into mainstream development. School health is all included in this social protection.

data.context_remarks:
This training manual is designed to help teachers understand and deliver integrated deworming and vision screening as part of the School Health Integrated Program (SHIP) for Liberia. The manual has an integrated approach and is intended to help teachers implement various school health interventions such as vision screening and deworming using available resources. It is not exhaustive but provides an insight for teachers to deliver simple, effective, and efficient integrated school health activities for school age children. It is expected as the Liberian educational system becomes familiar with an integrated approach to school health, additional interventions such as hearing screening, malaria education or oral health may possibly be added in similar manner.
The manual consists of two sections, the first section covers content for vision screening while the second section deals with deworming. Each section gives a background information and guidelines for the teacher to conduct vision screening and deworming accordingly using an integrated approach. The sections contain the objectives, strategies, steps and community engagement contents that will enable a teacher to engage their students in the classroom and the school community. 
In addition to the two sections, we have four appendices. Appendix one is the school register that captures the details of school age children and appendix two is the school summary sheet which will be used to summarize entries from the school register for reporting. Appendices three and four are the district and county summary forms, which are to be completed by the District Education Officers (DEOs) and the County Education Officers (CEOs) respectively.

data.context_remarks: The project has trained optometric technicians to add to the additional human resource and supplement the limited number of existing eye health workers in Liberia to process access to eye health counselling and advice which has strengthen the existing referral pathway to ensure children with other eye health conditions can access high quality services at designated referral centers. This includes children with pediatric cataract and other eye conditions identified during vision screening and sight testing.

data.policy_barrier_remarks: Eyeglasses not included in health insurance or other state financing mechanism: Limited budget allocation
data.institutional_barrier_remarks: The availability of eye glasses for children due to supply chain issue, the glasses are imported and not produce in country
data.supply_chain_enabler_keywords: eyeglasses, spectacle frames, lenses, optical product+ procure(ment), set up
data.supply_chain_enabler_remarks: 4.1 Eyeglasses: The Liberia Health Regulatory Agency regulates and direct the import, procurement of eye glasses. There is no specific regulation on the importation, production, procurement or sale of spectacle frames and lenses
data.supply_chain_barrier_remarks: 4.1 Eyeglasses: Eye glasses are not included on the MoH Essential supplies list.
data.supply_chain_strengths: Availability of spectacles at public health facilities (with a wide range of frames for children, adults, male, female, boys and girls) could address challenges linked to compliance and cost
data.supply_chain_limitations: The inclusion of eyeglasses  on the Ministry of Health Essential Supplies List  could facilitate importation and influence availability and affordability of spectacles.
data.barriers_keywords:
Eyeglasses+ not produce(d)
No+ eyeglasses+ available
data.barriers_remarks: 5.3: No eyeglasses suitable for children available within the intervention area: The availability of eye glasses for children due to supply chain issues
data.barriers_limitations: 5. Supply chain challenges that affect cost, range (frames) and availability of spectacles

data.supply_chain_enabler_remarks: 4.1 Eyeglasses: other supply chains for opticals: OneSight has set up optical products in health facilities in the intervention area.
data.supply_chain_limitations: People from the lowest income groups can not afford for spectacles despite efforts made to subsidize the cost.
data.barriers_remarks: 5.3: The availability of eye glasses for children due to supply chain issue, the glasses are imported and not produce in country
